speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Beds are clean, tea is questionable. You staying?

speaker: Player
text: Just passing through.

speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Then pass through warm. Wind bites at night.

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Mind the chalk lines—they’re a fierce tournament.

speaker: Player
text: What’s the prize?

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Bragging. Best currency in the yard.

speaker: Juno
text: Courier on a deadline—talk fast or walk with me.

speaker: Player
text: Any news?

speaker: Juno
text: Packages go missing near the docks. Someone’s skimming. Keep your eyes open.

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Papers, please. Ha—kidding. We’re short on papers today.

speaker: Player
text: Anything we should avoid?

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Back alley after midnight. Unless you like buying trouble twice.

speaker: Pasha
text: Step right up—today's lots are honest and only mildly cursed!

speaker: Player
text: We're just looking.

speaker: Pasha
text: Looking is free. Advice, too: bids rise when the drones clock off—late evenings are fierce.

speaker: Rana
text: Watch your step. Half these pallets wobble worse than a drunk loader.

speaker: Player
text: Busy day?

speaker: Rana
text: Always. If a crate's tagged in blue, it’s bound for the mine. Don’t touch blue.

speaker: Sedge
text: You look like someone who can mind a secret.

speaker: Player
text: Depends on the secret.

speaker: Sedge
text: Then it depends on the price. Come back when you’ve got something to trade.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: If you’re bleeding, sit. If you’re not, don’t start.

speaker: Player
text: We’re fine.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: Good. I’m running low on synth-gel and patience in equal measure.

speaker: Sima
text: Noodles hot, pepper hotter. Want steam or fire?

speaker: Player
text: Surprise me.

speaker: Sima
text: Bold. I like bold. Here—don’t breathe in too deep.

speaker: Tallo
text: New faces? Welcome. Roof leaks, hearts don’t.

speaker: Player
text: Nice place.

speaker: Tallo
text: It’s home. The trick is to keep it ours.

speaker: Ellie
text: Just swapping out panels before the sparks get ambitious. What do you need?

speaker: Ellie
text: The arcade's alive again! Here, a little something for keeping the lights bright.

speaker: Jed
text: Nova. Good. The Warden is sealing the lift.

speaker: Nova
text: Zeke found us a route. Come on.

speaker: Jed
text: The override won't hold itself. If I let go, the lift locks.

speaker: Nova
text: Then we jam it. Move your hand.

speaker: Jed
text: No time. Take the Chime. It should wake a pod.

speaker: Jed
text: You look awful. Sit down.

speaker: Nova
text: The conduit by my door was live. The repair ticket said closed.

speaker: Jed
text: Same stamp as the others. Give me your cutter.

speaker: Nova
text: I need it for shift.

speaker: Jed
text: I know. Eat this while I check the housing. Your kit is under the bench.

speaker: Jed
text: Cold loop's split. Bench is yours.

speaker: Nova
text: You kept a spare?

speaker: Jed
text: Kept it for you. Try not to melt this one.

speaker: Jed
text: Clean hum. Let me see your wrist.

speaker: Nova
text: It's fine, Jed.

speaker: Jed
text: Then get your clearance before the first cage drops.

speaker: Nova
text: I know.

speaker: Jed
text: Checkpoint first. If the scanner stalls, find Zeke at the booth.

speaker: Jed
text: Grab a seat if you're sticking around. If something starts smoking, hand me the wrench first.

speaker: Jed
text: You made it back in one piece. That surge settle down once you pulled the core?

speaker: Jed
text: I'll open the housing and see where the signal leads. Good work bringing it back.

speaker: Jed
text: Town runs smoother thanks to you. Keep pace like that and we'll stay ahead of the scrappers.

speaker: Jed
text: Stellarium core is cooling on my workbench thanks to you. We'll make good use of it.

speaker: Jed
text: Smell that ozone? That's the core spool. Hand it over before it cooks through your belt.

speaker: Jed
text: Track that pulse to its source. If the deep mine lit up, something below Sector 4 is chewing through power.

speaker: Jed
text: Nice work. That's all of 'em. Now, get this gear back to their owners. They've been pestering me for days.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Quiet feet, please. The little ones are mapping constellations they have never been allowed to see.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Wonderful! The kids will be back under the stars tonight. Please, take this for your trouble.

speaker: Maddie
text: Beans are roasting, and for once the grinder is not threatening to explode. Did you need something?

speaker: Maddie
text: You fixed it! Oh, thank you! Take these credits - may your next cup be on me.

speaker: Nova
text: Burrower was feeding on it. Nest is quiet now, but whoever rigged the chamber knew what they were doing.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Badge.

speaker: Nova
text: Morning to you too.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: No badge, no entry. Try the booth.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Badge ready before you reach the scanner.

speaker: Zeke
text: I can't open the file until Hank logs the rejection.

speaker: Zeke
text: Name, ID, clearance... Oh. You.

speaker: Nova
text: I need the pass. Shift starts in ten.

speaker: Zeke
text: Right. One moment.

speaker: Zeke
text: Your file says mandatory retirement. Bio-reclamation.

speaker: Nova
text: What does that mean?

speaker: Zeke
text: They kill you and reclaim the cost.

speaker: Zeke
text: I can change the cause. It has to blame the grid, not you.

speaker: Zeke
text: Operator error leaves your name on it. Try again.

speaker: Zeke
text: Unauthorized modification implies intent. We need an equipment failure.

speaker: Zeke
text: Grid instability. The override is temporary, but you're alive. Take the badge. Logistics will review the rest.

speaker: Zeke
text: Not here. The booth records audio.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You opened it? No alarm? No sweep team? Hah. They really did forget the dead.

speaker: Nova
text: They scratched names into the lid.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Keep the names to yourself. Take the grenades. You didn't get them from me.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You want something useful, cutter girl? There's a cache behind the strip. Old resistance stock.

speaker: Nova
text: Why not grab it yourself?

speaker: Scrapper
text: Because I like breathing. Find the crate, open it clean, and bring me proof it wasn't a trap.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Keep them dry and away from the cheap detonators.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Keep your voice down.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Buying, selling, or just looking guilty? Keep it quick either way.

speaker: Doc
text: The coughing stopped. What did you find?

speaker: Nova
text: The fans were reversed. I cleared the blockage.

speaker: Doc
text: Good. Take these corrosive rounds. I have no use for them in here.

speaker: Doc
text: Don't inhale too deep. The vents are backing poison into my patients again.

speaker: Nova
text: Again?

speaker: Doc
text: Dominion denies repairs faster than I can file them. Patch the cracked vent before the ward turns blue.

speaker: Doc
text: Air's still bad. At least it isn't poisoning the ward.

speaker: Doc
text: If you're bleeding, sit. If you're not, don't start.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Nova. Your temporary badge landed on my review queue.

speaker: Nova
text: Zeke said my file got routed to Logistics review.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Sector 4 needs a cutter. Pass shield training and I'll clear the lift.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Loading Dock. Clear the cargo and finish the shield drill.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: The training log is still blank. Finish the shield drill.

speaker: Nova
text: And if I don't?

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Then the lift stays locked and your override expires.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Training's logged. Good. Now we talk Sector 4.

speaker: Nova
text: You said the Deep Elevator opens after the shield training.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: It does. Sector 4's relays are failing, and you're the cutter I have.

speaker: Nova
text: Sector 4? That's deep mine. The lift is locked.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: The lift is open. Restore the junction power, then trace the failed relays.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: You. Cutter girl. Loader's sitting idle, cargo's blocking my schedule, and your certification log is blank.

speaker: Nova
text: There are workers behind that pile.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Then move the pile. Finish the shield drill while you're there.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Route's clear. Training is logged. Report when you're ready for Sector 4.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Company time ain't charity. Move.

speaker: Miner Bill
text: Bar's quiet if you ignore the vents. I recommend ignoring the vents.

speaker: The Warden
text: Nova. Step away from the launch controls.

speaker: Player
text: No.

speaker: The Warden
text: The shield is locked to this colony. You will not cross it.

speaker: Zeke
text: Warden's down. Security will reboot soon.

speaker: Player
text: Get the hatch. I have the Chime.

speaker: Zeke
text: Inside. I'll seal the hatch.

speaker: Zeke
text: The console has a matching port. Try the Chime.

speaker: Player
text: Here goes.

speaker: Zeke
text: It took. Core is live. Start the launch sequence.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova... *cough*... My head feels like a reactor breach. Are you intact?

speaker: Nova
text: I'm fine, Zeke. The pod took the brunt of the impact. How are you holding up?

speaker: Zeke
text: Everything is bruised, but my suit's recycler is still pumping. We're on the surface, Nova. We actually crossed the barrier... but the pod's core is completely fried.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the core housing in the wreckage. If the Chime is still intact, we need to recover it before this swamp water short-circuits it completely.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the wreckage of the pod core. We can't leave the Chime behind.

speaker: Zeke
text: That kit from the pod should keep me upright. Patch me up, then we follow the glow-water together.

speaker: Zeke
text: The stream of glow-water seems to flow deeper into the concrete structures. Let's move before the wreck draws company.

speaker: Zeke
text: The beacon's pulse is strong. It's coming from that massive stone gate up ahead. But the path is covered in thick razor-vines. Be careful, Nova.

speaker: Zeke
text: The pod is not asking for a password. It is asking for the room to stop fighting itself. Read the murals, map the pod pressure, check the overview sockets, and stabilize the coolant before you touch the ring console.

speaker: Zeke
text: The chamber finally sounds like one machine. The rings are locking into alignment, and the pod is venting pressure cleanly.

speaker: Zeke
text: No, the rings are still oscillating. Something in the chamber is still pushing against the rest of the system.

speaker: Zeke
text: Fascinating scans! The cellular structure of these crystalline ferns behaves like an acoustic chamber. Here, take some spare stabilizers for your help.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova, this surface is incredible. The plants here respond directly to resonance frequencies. If you scan 5 unique flora specimens, I can compile an ecological catalog. Can you do that?

speaker: Orion
text: This barrier is not a lock so much as a living circuit in pain. Steady the horn, ground the cup, read the pressure roots, and starve the breakers. Then the acoustic lock will accept a resolving tone.

speaker: Orion
text: There. The system can breathe again, and the barrier has nothing left to hold.

speaker: Orion
text: The barrier hums louder, pushing us back. One of its bands is still being forced out of tune.

speaker: Orion
text: All three crystal voices are seated. The mural is ready for the resonator columns; let the room carry the chord instead of forcing it by hand.

speaker: Orion
text: The mural stabilizes: many minds, distinct as Ice, carried without dissolving in the Ocean.

speaker: Orion
text: No, the colors are shifting erratically. The room is missing one of its voices.

speaker: Orion
text: Nova, Tideglass Beach is nearby. The wet shore grows herbs that can bind Source residue into something useful. Gather the clean materials and I'll show you how to make resin that will not poison your hands.

speaker: Orion
text: The stasis mural is missing three resonance crystals from the Sanctuary wings. Recover their voices, then we can align the chord without letting the Ocean overwhelm us.

speaker: Orion
text: The current of this place is strong, Nova. I can hear the ocean's resonance.

speaker: Gh0st
text: You do not belong on this frequency. My orders are simple: erase the anomaly.

speaker: Player
text: Wait, we aren't here to fight. The Warden sent us!

speaker: Gh0st
text: The Warden... *static*... error. System logic loop detected. Harmonic lock override...

speaker: Gh0st
text: The transmitter is beyond an active vent security grid. Blind its timing, pass the guard systems without an alert, then retrieve the core. You can build something faster from its timing crystal.

speaker: Gh0st
text: My blades are quiet, but my sensors are alert.

speaker: Zeke
text: Welcome to my safehouse... or what's left of it. They scrubbed my lease, my work history, my access tags. But the pipes still know me, and the walls still carry my old wiring.

speaker: Nova
text: Then we use what they forgot to erase. Orion, shield the room. If the city hears us breathing, the heist dies before it starts.

speaker: Orion
text: Establishing resonance lock. There. The Source is quiet here. The city will hear only rain, faulty neon, and its own arrogance.

speaker: Jax
text: You want the Upper City? Then you want six things: timing, credentials, a route, a costume, a terminal pull, and the nerve to look bored while people decide whether you count as human.

options.text: Yes, Zeke said you have the schedules.

speaker: Jax
text: Schedules are on this drive. The rich don't guard what workers touch, so start there: underrail map, plaza kiosk, service gate, safehouse roof laundry lines, then Zeke's workstation. Bring it all back to the planning table before courage turns stupid.

speaker: Jax
text: The neon sign outside is dead. Can't run a bar in the dark, and I don't mean ambience. Patrols ignore a place that looks open and watched. Bring me a neon core from the street sweepers, and I'll make it worth your while.

options.text: I'll get a core for you.

speaker: Jax
text: You got the core. Install it on the sign console over there.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Nova. Dominion databases at the Transit Plaza might hold records on... Elara. My sister. If I touch the terminal, old flags may wake up. If you search it, maybe I learn whether grief has an address.

speaker: System
text: Downloading Spire security archives... deleted transfer chain recovered. Elara was not lost. She was routed.

speaker: Mika
text: Try the skewers! Real protein today, I promise. No recycled paste. Also... can you help me fix these lanterns? When the lights fail, patrols call us a hazard and clear the stalls.

speaker: Mika
text: Just redirect the primary power coupling to light them up. Nothing heroic. Just enough glow to remind people the market is still ours.

speaker: System
text: Zeke's workstation accepts the stolen timing, badge pattern, disguise notes, and plaza pull. The old employee profile it builds from his erased life compiles into a service-elevator route.

speaker: System
text: The planning table lights every thread at once: patrol timing, badges, uniforms, security records, and the elevator route. Nova sees the weak part of the whole system: it depends on everyone staying in their assigned place.

speaker: Curator
text: Fascinating refraction. Sector 9 lineage? No, not lineage. Salvage.

speaker: Curator
text: A Starborn stray in guest light. Control your pet, or I will call sanitation and security in whichever order is more entertaining.

speaker: Zeke
text: Sir, this is a commemorative installation. Very lifelike. Very expensive. Touch it again and I will have to invent paperwork neither of us wants to read.

speaker: Zeke
text: Standard Dominion scanner grid, routed through Laundry Service because the people who clean their lives are invisible by design. That makes us ugly, useful math.

speaker: Zeke
text: You deleted my employee ID? Fine. I am deleting your admin privileges. Resignation accepted.

speaker: Orion
text: The Prism Gallery is not solving light. It is forcing pressure to become visible. Dominion wrapped a cage in beauty and called it scholarship. Read the containment field, the prism shutters, and the tether-line routing before touching the Lens.

speaker: Orion
text: When those three systems agree, the containment field will open because the room understands itself again. Do not mistake permission for mercy.

speaker: Zeke
text: Wait. Energy density is spiking before you even touch it. Nova, it is reading you back. If you grab that thing, the feedback loop lights us up like a flare.

speaker: Nova
text: We did not come this far to ask a cage for permission. I can tune it out.

speaker: Thorne
text: You just tripped a station-wide audit. Do you have any idea what you have done, or are you still calling this courage?

speaker: Thorne
text: He is coming. And he does not leave survivors. I cannot let him find you here. He is not city security. He is what city security fears.

speaker: Vale
text: Anomaly detected in the Archive. Administrator, lock the district. Seal the drone test alcove and the roof. I want no ungoverned noise leaving Sector 0, especially if it has learned to sing.

speaker: Concierge
text: Welcome to the altitude where price is proof of citizenship.

speaker: Lab Terminal
text: Prototype trial armed. Warning: liability has been transferred to the test subject.

speaker: Gh0st
text: I know where the vents are. I crawled through them when I was small.

speaker: Zeke
text: These vents are running hot enough to cook a tank. Give me thirty seconds and a reason to hate this place more.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Project Phantom. That name is on the inside of my skull.

speaker: Nova
text: Open it. We face what it says together.

speaker: Gh0st
text: My kill-suite reads Spectral IDs. When you channel the Source, you ring close enough to Elara that the failsafe grabs the wheel.

speaker: Gh0st
text: If I ever draw on you again, use this override key. No hesitation.

speaker: Vale
text: Sector 7 is under audit. Unregistered craft detected. No departures until the anomaly is resolved.

speaker: Gh0st
text: These belts carried bodies before they carried machines. Watch the timing.

speaker: Gh0st
text: They made more of me. Faster. Emptier. Put them down before the line teaches them pain.

speaker: Orion
text: The Forge is breathing in rhythm. Belts, crushers, then silence. Find what controls the silence.

speaker: Nova
text: It wants to build. I can feel it pulling shape out of me.

speaker: Rylos
text: You are malfunctioning, Unit 734. Submit for correction. Pain is just data leaving the body.

speaker: Vale
text: Commander Rylos. Audit complete. Denied. Director Thorne is relieved. All sectors: lockdown.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Vale locked the Ring to Thorne's old cipher. I know the scar tissue in this code. Give me ten seconds.

speaker: Maintenance Bot
text: SOLAR ARRAY OCCLUDED. HORTICULTURAL LOSSES EXCEED EXECUTIVE PRESENTATION LIMITS.

speaker: Thorne
text: I built the cage. I thought Harmony would be controllable. Sellable. Vale is not trying to own the world. He is trying to erase every voice but his.

speaker: Vale
text: Welcome to the Ring. Director Thorne is unavailable. Orbital Compliance is acting command. For your safety, remain where you are.

speaker: Thorne
text: Three firewall nodes. I designed the server route to keep everyone else out. Vale changed the locks and left me inside it. Map the coolant pulse first, then follow Alpha, Beta, Gamma in order.

speaker: Elara
text: 734. You are late. I cannot hold the door and the firewall. Take the Anchor. Get them out. That is a direct order, brother.

speaker: Vale
text: A choir still contains variance. Variance contains pain. One voice. A solo.

speaker: Zeke
text: I kept telling myself I was surviving the system. I quit. And I do not need to be useful to deserve this family.

speaker: Gh0st
text: I could not save her. I can stop earning my right to exist by bleeding. No more orders.

speaker: Orion
text: They are not dead. They are still in the current. I was frozen. Now I have to remain on the shore a little longer.

speaker: Vale
text: Thorne wanted to bottle the ocean. I will break the glass. No bodies to starve. No minds to break. Why do you struggle to add the friction back?

speaker: Nova
text: You are not saving them. You are building a mass grave and calling the silence peace.

speaker: Sentinel-3
text: [High-pitched tone]... Trading interface established. Select components for exchange.

speaker: Sentinel-3
text: Core status: 89% coherence. Atmospheric humidity: high. The canopy resonance is vibrating at key G minor today.

speaker: Zeke
text: Patrol beacons scatter their signatures across the Wilds. Triangulate all three before trusting the final vector. Their cutter team may have carried something useful.

speaker: Orion
text: Rest is not surrender. The Ocean presses against every thought; food, trusted voices, and a Neural Stabilizer give the Ice time to hold its shape.

speaker: Orion
text: Those razor-vines carry toxic spores. A heated edge could cauterize them as it cuts. The lost patrol transceiver may lead us to a cutter you can repair.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Do not touch the transmitter yet. First blind the security grid on the gantry, then cross the grate between guard sweeps.
